mission.title: Install and Configure Kaito on Kubernetes
mission.description: KAITO is a Kubernetes AI Toolchain Operator that automates the AI/ML model inference or tuning workload in a Kubernetes cluster. It simplifies the deployment of large AI inference models, providing features like auto-provisioning of GPU nodes and autoscaling based on service metrics.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KAITO Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kaito https://kaito-project.github.io/kaito/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KAITO using Helm
steps.description:
Install the KAITO operator in a new namespace called 'kaito'.
```bash
helm install kaito kaito/kaito --namespace kaito --create-namespace --version v0.9.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the KAITO pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kaito
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for KAITO
steps.description:
Edit the KAITO deployment to set resource limits for the pods.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kaito --namespace kaito
# Add the following under spec.template.spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "1000m"
#     memory: "2Gi"
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KAITO Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall kaito --namespace kaito
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions
uninstall.description:
Delete the KAITO CRDs to remove any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete crd workspaces.kaito.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kaito' namespace to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kaito
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current KAITO configuration and resources for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kaito -o yaml > kaito-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Make sure your Helm repository is up to date before upgrading.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KAITO
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the KAITO installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade kaito kaito/kaito --namespace kaito --version v0.9.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the KAITO pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kaito
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
This may indicate insufficient resources in the cluster. Check the events for the pods:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kaito
``` 
If resources are insufficient, consider adding more nodes or adjusting resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: KAITO controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the KAITO controller pod for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <kaito-controller-pod-name> --namespace kaito
``` 
If there are configuration errors, review the deployment settings.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to CRDs, ensure they are installed:
```bash
kubectl get crd
``` 
If missing, reinstall the KAITO Helm chart.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KAITO will result in the KAITO operator running in the 'kaito' namespace with all pods in a running state. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are healthy.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kaito https://kaito-project.github.io/kaito/charts
helm repo update
helm install kaito kaito/kaito --namespace kaito --create-namespace --version v0.9.0

kubectl edit deployment kaito --namespace kaito
# Add the following under spec.template.spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "1000m"
#     memory: "2Gi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy KAITO.
